title: Why Rocket Lab Stock Is Rocketing Higher This Week
text: Rocket Lab's stock has experienced a significant surge this week following the company's first-quarter 2026 financial results announcement on May 7. The launch services provider's shares reached an all-time high of $133.18, representing a 25.7% increase from the prior Friday's close. The catalyst for investor enthusiasm stems from better-than-expected earnings. While analysts anticipated quarterly revenue of $189.7 million, Rocket Lab delivered $200.4 million representing a record for quarterly sales. The company also achieved notable milestones including a 38.2% gross margin and a substantial backlog of $2.2 billion. Analyst support has intensified this momentum. Deutsche Bank maintained its buy rating while increasing its price target by 64% to $120 per share. Additionally, Clear Street initiated coverage with a buy recommendation and a $150 price target. For the upcoming quarter, management projects revenue between $225 million and $240 million with a gross margin ranging from 33% to 35%. However, valuation concerns exist. The company trades at over 108 times sales, significantly exceeding its five-year average multiple of 14.6. This premium valuation may lead investors seeking space economy exposure toward alternative options within the sector.
